mission.title: prometheus: checksum mismatch breaking Grafana queries
mission.description: checksum mismatch breaking Grafana queries. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify prometheus troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your prometheus deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
**What did you do?**
Updated Prometheus 2.12.0 to 2.15.0. Look at queries in grafana. Sometimes graphs show errors like:

Usually seems to happen only when I had a long time window. The same error occurs on all queries intermittently.

steps.title: Apply the fix for checksum mismatch breaking Grafana queries
steps.description:
Yup, we have race on `s.crc32` and `s.buf` on chunk file reader. https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/pull/6514 fixed most of it, s.buf is still to remove (: 

 are you on it? Can take it over. We might need patch release for this.
```yaml
enableAdminAPI: false
    image: docker.io/prom/prometheus:v2.15.0
    podMetadata:
      annotations:
        sidecar.istio.io/inject: "false"
      labels:
        app: prometheus
    resources:
      requests:
        memory: 32Gi
    retention: 72h
    scrapeInterval: 15s
    secrets:
    - istio.prometheus
    securityContext:
      fsGroup: 2000
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 1000
    serviceAccountName: prometheus
    serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector:
      any: true
    serviceMonitorSelector:
      any: true
    storage:
      volumeClaimTemplate:
        selector: {}
      
```

steps.title: Confirm checksum mismatch breaking Grafana queries is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Yup, we have race on `s.crc32` and `s.buf` on chunk file reader. https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/pull/6514 fixed most of it, s.buf is still to remove (: 

 are you on it? Can take it over. We might need patch release for this.
codeSnippets:
enableAdminAPI: false
    image: docker.io/prom/prometheus:v2.15.0
    podMetadata:
      annotations:
        sidecar.istio.io/inject: "false"
      labels:
        app: prometheus
    resources:
      requests:
        memory: 32Gi
    retention: 72h
    scrapeInterval: 15s
    secrets:
    - istio.prometheus
    securityContext:
      fsGroup: 2000
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 1000
    serviceAccountName: prometheus
    serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector:
      any: true
    serviceMonitorSelector:
      any: true
    storage:
      volumeClaimTemplate:
        selector: {}
        spec:
          accessModes:
          - ReadWriteOnce
          resources:
            requests:
              storage: 500Gi
          storageClassName: ssd
    version: v2.15.0
for TIME in {1577173160..1577174660}; do curl "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=sum(increase(gateway_wan_bytes_received%5B30d%5D))&start=${TIME}&end=$((TIME+300))&step=15"; done;

for TIME in {1577173500..1577174000}; do curl "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=sum(increase(gateway_wan_bytes_sent%5B5y%5D))%2F(sum(increase(gateway_wan_bytes_received%5B5y%5D))%2Bsum(increase(gateway_wan_bytes_sent%5B5y%5D)))&start=${TIME}&end=$((TIME+300))&step=15"; done;

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
